title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. villosissima
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. villosissima, also known as Woolly Gray Santolina or Woolly Cotton Lavender, is a variety of the gray santolina plant, which is a low-growing, shrubby perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote a good night's sleep. Santolina chamaecyparissus var. villosissima can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in potpourri and other herbal remedies.
